description: You are a molecular and cellular biologist designing antisense oligonucleotide probes for an in situ hybridization experiment to detect human insulin mRNA in pancreatic beta cells. Using the human insulin GenBank file located at ~/UGENE_Data/human_insulin_gene.gb, extract the exact coding sequence (CDS) and generate its reverse complement to serve as the antisense probe template. Export the sense CDS sequence to ~/UGENE_Data/results/antisense_probe/insulin_cds_sense.fasta and the antisense sequence to ~/UGENE_Data/results/antisense_probe/insulin_cds_antisense.fasta. Create a probe design report at ~/UGENE_Data/results/antisense_probe/probe_design_report.txt containing the exact length of the CDS in nucleotides, the GC content percentage, the first 30 nucleotides of both the sense and antisense strands, and a note stating whether the GC content is suitable for hybridization (ideal range is 40-60%).

metadata.data_source: NCBI GenBank (human insulin gene NM_000207.3 or J00265.1)
